title: Aureline delete-request state record
description: Boundary schema for delete_request_state_record entries used by retention/deletion matrix consumers, support and admin handoff flows, and the privacy-history layer to render the per-instance state of a delete request without pretending the deletion already finished. Each record names the stable delete state, expected next state-change chronology, remaining-location disclosure, partial blockers (policy, sync backlog, provider backlog, legal hold, etc.), and the export/handoff posture that lets support and admin flows share retention and delete state without exposing unrelated private payloads. Stable delete states (delete_requested, policy_retention, legal_hold, delete_completed, exported_copy_remains_local) are reused verbatim across privacy, collaboration, support, and offboarding flows so held or policy-retained data never masquerades as successful deletion. Adding a stable_delete_state, partial_blocker_class, remaining_location_class, handoff_audience, or expected_next_state_change_class is additive-minor and bumps delete_request_state_schema_version; repurposing a value is breaking and opens a decision row.

delete_request_state_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the delete-request-state vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

stable_ref.description: Opaque, stable reference safe to log, render, and cite. Raw payloads, raw credentials, raw policy bundle bytes, raw prompts, and raw hold justification bytes never appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable prose. It must not contain raw payload bodies, raw paths, raw prompts, raw credentials, or raw policy bundle bytes.

chronology.description: Chronology packet for one timestamp. Mirrors the wall-clock/UTC/timezone shape used elsewhere in governance schemas so renderers do not re-derive offsets.

stable_delete_state.description: Frozen stable delete-state vocabulary reused verbatim across privacy, collaboration, support, and offboarding flows. delete_requested = a request was accepted; no completion is implied. policy_retention = a stricter retention floor keeps at least one managed or audit subset; this is not successful deletion. legal_hold = a hold blocks destructive lifecycle steps; this is not successful deletion. delete_completed = the requested destructive action completed for the named scope (receipts and tombstones may remain as metadata-only evidence). exported_copy_remains_local = the product produced or downloaded an export copy that remains under user/device control and is outside the managed delete job.

stable_state_label.description: Required user-facing label for the stable delete state. The label MUST agree with stable_delete_state; surfaces that render a chip read this string verbatim and MUST NOT substitute synonyms ('removed', 'gone', 'private', 'sent', 'complete').

completion_claim.description: Frozen completion-claim vocabulary. delete_completed_with_retained_metadata is reserved for receipts/tombstones that are not hidden payload copies; delete_completed_with_retained_subset is reserved when a class subset stays under policy retention or legal hold and the surface MUST surface that subset rather than reporting full destruction. exported_copy_remains_no_managed_delete is the completion claim when the request was strictly an export and no managed delete was attempted.

actor_class.description: Frozen actor-class vocabulary. Mirrors export_delete_request_summary so privacy-history and matrix consumers share one actor enum.

actor.description: Actor descriptor. Never carries raw emails, names, tokens, or directory identifiers.

source_flow.description: Frozen source-flow vocabulary. Names the surface or flow that produced or last updated the delete-request state.

scope_class.description: Frozen scope vocabulary. Names what the request operates on; surfaces MUST NOT silently widen a single-record scope to user_account or organization.

scope_refs.description: Stable refs for the scoped subjects. Raw subject bodies never appear here.

matrix_row_refs.description: row_id values from the retention/deletion matrix this request affects. Every record cites at least one row so the user-visible explanation of what data exists, who owns retention, and how delete behaves is anchored to typed posture.

scope.description: Scope of the delete request. matrix_row_refs binds the request to retention/deletion matrix rows so the answer to 'what is this delete trying to remove' is always typed.

expected_next_state_change_class.description: Frozen expected-next-state-change vocabulary. definite_at_chronology = the next state change is scheduled and a chronology is required. by_chronology_or_sooner = a deadline by which the change MUST occur. after_*_backlog_clears = a queue or provider backlog is the gate. on_hold_review_or_clear / on_policy_floor_reached = a hold or retention floor is the gate. no_further_state_change_expected = the record is terminal for this request. indeterminate_pending_admin = an admin or operator action is required and a chronology cannot be promised; surfaces MUST render this as 'requires admin action' rather than implying a clock.

expected_at.description: Expected chronology of the next state change. Required when change_class is definite_at_chronology or by_chronology_or_sooner; MUST NOT be present when change_class is no_further_state_change_expected or indeterminate_pending_admin.

gate_ref.description: Stable ref to the queue, hold, retention policy, or admin task that gates the next state change. Required when the change_class names a backlog, hold, policy floor, or export window.

allOf.description: Definite chronology change classes require expected_at.
Backlog, hold, policy-floor, and export-window classes require gate_ref.
Terminal and indeterminate classes MUST NOT carry expected_at.

expected_next_state_change.description: Expected next state-change disclosure. Surfaces that show 'next update' or 'expected by' text resolve through this object so dishonest 'should be quick' phrasing is structurally prevented.

remaining_location_class.description: Frozen remaining-location vocabulary. local_device_only = only the originating device still holds the payload. local_export_copy = a downloaded export copy remains under user control. managed_archive_held / managed_archive_policy_retained = a managed archive still holds the payload under hold or policy retention. managed_archive_replicated_pending_purge = a managed archive is propagating delete to replicas and the request is waiting for that purge. destruction_receipt_only = only a metadata-only receipt remains. import_source_origin = the only remaining copy lives at the import origin and is outside Aureline policy. no_remaining_location = nothing remains.

matrix_row_ref.description: row_id from the retention/deletion matrix that owns this remaining-location explanation.

artifact_refs.description: Stable refs to the remaining artifacts (export file id, managed archive id, replica id, receipt id). Empty when no per-artifact id is available.

honesty_note.description: Honesty sentence the surface MUST render verbatim alongside the location list. Free-text alternatives that imply nothing remains are non-conforming.

remaining_location_disclosure.description: Per-location disclosure of what remains. A delete-request state of policy_retention, legal_hold, or exported_copy_remains_local MUST list at least one non-no_remaining_location entry.

partial_blocker_class.description: Frozen partial-blocker vocabulary. Distinguishes policy/hold blockers (policy_retention_floor, legal_hold, support_investigation, export_pending_hold) from infrastructure delays (sync_backlog, provider_backlog, managed_service_unavailable) and from out-of-scope conditions (manual_local_capture_required, outside_platform_scope, import_source_unreachable). A blocker that cannot be typed denies with partial_blocker_class_unresolved rather than collapsing to a generic 'pending'.

service_surface_ref.description: Service or provider surface ref when blocker_class is sync_backlog, provider_backlog, or managed_service_unavailable. Not present otherwise.

expected_clear_at.description: Expected clearance chronology. Null when the blocker has no scheduled clearance time (e.g. manual review, indefinite legal hold).

partial_blocker.description: One partial-result blocker. Surfaces that explain why a delete is delayed, partial, or impossible right now read these rows verbatim instead of inventing 'still working on it' phrasing.

handoff_audience.description: Frozen handoff-audience vocabulary. Names who the export/handoff packet is meant for so support and admin sharing cannot accidentally widen disclosure. external_compliance_reviewer rows MUST cite the redaction profile the packet is built against.

handoff_export_class.description: Frozen handoff-export class vocabulary. matrix_state_only_packet exports only the typed retention/delete state and matrix row refs (no payloads). matrix_state_plus_redacted_summary adds reviewable redacted summaries. support_handoff_packet / admin_audit_packet / compliance_export_packet add the linkages each audience requires under their redaction profile.

exposes_unrelated_private_payloads.description: Honesty invariant: a handoff packet MUST NOT include payloads outside the matrix rows in scope. The constant is false; surfaces that fail this assertion are rejected.

redaction_profile_ref.description: Repo-relative reference to the redaction profile the packet is built against.

included_matrix_row_refs.description: row_id values included in this handoff packet. MUST be a subset of the request's scope.matrix_row_refs.

excluded_matrix_row_refs.description: row_id values intentionally excluded from this handoff packet (e.g. raw secret rows excluded from a support handoff).

handoff_packet.description: Export/handoff packet descriptor. Emitted when support or admin shares retention and delete state. The handoff cannot widen scope past the matrix rows in the request and MUST cite a redaction profile.

audit_linkage.description: Audit linkage. Mirrors export_delete_request_summary's audit_linkage so a delete-request state record and its summary share the same linkage shape.

honesty_assertions.description: Honesty invariants every delete_request_state_record MUST assert. A record that fails any constant=true invariant is rejected.

source_ref.description: Stable ref of the surface that owns the request (privacy delete sheet id, support case id, offboarding flow id).

partial_blockers.description: Partial-result blockers that explain why the request is delayed, partial, or impossible right now. Empty array is legal only when stable_delete_state is delete_completed and no retained subset exists, or when stable_delete_state is delete_requested and no blocker has yet applied.

handoff_packets.description: Handoff packets emitted to share retention/delete state with support, admin, or compliance audiences. Empty array means no handoff has been emitted; surfaces MUST NOT silently emit a handoff without listing it here.

__fixture__.description: Optional fixture annotation block; never part of the runtime record.

allOf.description: legal_hold MUST carry at least one legal_hold partial blocker and MUST NOT claim full destruction.
policy_retention MUST carry at least one policy_retention_floor or support_investigation blocker and MUST NOT claim full destruction.
exported_copy_remains_local MUST cite at least one local_export_copy remaining-location entry.
delete_completed completion claims must align with the delete_completed state.

delete_request_state_record.description: One delete-request state record. Surfaces explaining why a deletion is delayed, partial, or impossible right now resolve to exactly one record per request and re-render whenever a stable_delete_state, expected_next_state_change, remaining_location_disclosure, or partial_blocker changes.
